title: Anti-aircraft gun
content:
{{Top|leg}}
[[File:Artillery.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[B1-series battle droid/Legends|Battle droids]] operating an anti-aircraft gun during the [[Battle of Sarrish/Legends|Battle of Sarrish]].]]

'''Anti-aircraft guns''', also known as '''anti-aircraft artillery''' or '''anti-craft turrets''', were [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] emplacements stationed on battlefields, such as [[Mygeeto/Legends|Mygeeto]], to neutralize air targets in the area. They were especially useful in the defense of landing platforms. [[Clone trooper/Legends|Clone troopers]] fighting in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] used the slang term '''triple-A''' when referring to anti-aircraft artillery during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==History==
At the start of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], an AA gun was used to protect a Lucrehulk-class core ship. The clone commandos of [[Delta Squad/Legends|Delta Squad]] destroyed the AA gun, and then proceeded to disable the core ship. Several more were mounted on platforms during the [[Battle of Bespin (Clone Wars)|Battle of Bespin]]. The [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] version was used to shoot down incoming [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|Vulture droids]] and [[Mechanized Assault Flyer|MAF]] gunships, until they were taken over by [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] and used against [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7 ''Aethersprite''-class light interceptors]] and [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|LAAT/is]].

They were also used against the [[212th Attack Battalion/Legends|212th Attack Battalion]] and the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] at the [[Battle of Utapau/Legends|Battle of Utapau]]. There were two mounted at the mouth of the main hangar bay of ''[[Unlimited Projection]]'', and were used mostly against the [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|LAAT/i]] [[Gunship/Legends|gunships]] that were attempting to land reinforcements and assist in taking down the [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] forces stationed therein.<ref name="BFII">[[Star Wars Battlefront II: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Battlefront II'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref><ref name="RotS">[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> Members of the 501st Legion destroyed the ones in the hangar,<ref name="BFII" /> and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] destroyed two other turrets by taking out their controls.<ref name="RotS" />

They were also common during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] both the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] and the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] used AA turrets to destroy aerial vehicles. Numerous other factions used AA such as the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]]. The Empire utilized the advantages of the AA on their [[All Terrain Anti Aircraft/Legends|AT-AA]].

[[File:Eaw_at-aa_perspective.jpg|thumb|150px|An AT-AA]]

{{Weapon-stub}}
